stem: A cloud computing server cluster uses 3 parallel nodes for redundancy. The request served requires at least one node to function. node 1 has a reliability between 63% and 76% (uncertain due to limited operational data). node 2 has a reliability of 92%. node 3 has a reliability of 79%. What is the overall probability that the request served?

ground_truth.answer: P(request served) ranges from 0.9938 to 0.996. Point estimate: 0.995.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 nodes fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.37 × 0.08 × 0.21 = 0.0062. P(works) = 0.9938.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.24 × 0.08 × 0.21 = 0.004. P(works) = 0.996.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
